He should hoist a flag of very fine yarn and black colour, four
cubits in length, on a staff 12 cubits high. After understanding thoro-
ughly the different directions, he should ascertain the direction of
the wind through the movement of the streamer, at the Moon's
conjunction with Rohiiji.
[1 Danda—I Dhanus=4 Cubits.
vpi ^nvft fOT%r7*r!*rrs'T^: n
The method of finding out the quarters is explained in the
q. fe. IV. 19
The use of a flag for ascertaining the direction of the wind is
a matter to be specially taken note of, because even at modern
meteorological stations some kind of mechanical vane is employed.

252 Bxhat Samhitn
We may also remember what Kalidasa says about the wind and a
fine flag in the Saknntala ;
